violations.text: Chicken and potatoes were observed held below 135 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated food in microwave and turned hot hold unit up. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/10/2025
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RDIOH. Establishment has submitted CFSM paperwork to DOH. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 04/22/2024
The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety with RIDOH. Application printed. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2024

Chicken and vegetables were observed held at 127-132 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Per Temp logs food was received above 135. The unit that had food out of temperature was the only unit without a cover. Cover was added to unit. Product was also double panned. Second pan was removed to allow product to be in more direct contact with steam. Lunch to be served shortly. Establishment to take food temps around 10:00 until re-inspection to ensure product is above 135. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2024
